message:
test(storage): structure-aware fuzz harness for FTS5 search path

Adds tests/storage_fts_fuzz.rs — a proptest-based structure-aware fuzzer
for `StorageHandle::search_with_results`. The harness covers the FTS5
query edge the user can reach directly (queries thread through FTS5's
`MATCH` operator, which has its own grammar: double-quoted phrases,
`AND`/`OR`/`NOT`, prefix `word*`, column filters `col:term`, etc.).

Grammar fragments (`arb_token`, `arb_operand`, `arb_binary`,
`arb_malformed`, `arb_query`) compose realistic hostile inputs:
 * ascii words, digit-mixed words, snake/kebab tokens
 * unicode latin extended (café, naïve), CJK (漢字), emoji (🔥), Greek (Ω)
 * BOM prefix (U+FEFF), zero-width space (U+200B)
 * control chars (BEL, ANSI SGR)
 * NUL bytes, both standalone and smuggled mid-token
 * double-quoted phrases + nested/unterminated quote variants
 * prefix wildcards, column filters with both valid and bogus columns
 * bare/trailing/leading operators, unbalanced parens
 * a 4 KiB single-token tokenizer-stress case

Invariants asserted across 5,000 generated cases per test:
  (1) fuzz_search_never_panics_and_always_yields_structured_error —
      every input settles to Ok(Vec<SearchResult>) or
      Err(Storage(FtsQueryError | Database)). No panics, no foreign
      error variants.
  (2) fuzz_nul_byte_in_query_does_not_crash_engine — NUL bytes embedded
      in a query do not crash the FTS5 engine. An earlier version of
      this test asserted NUL *must* yield an error; the harness
      surfaced that premise as wrong: SQLite text bindings are
      length-prefixed (not C-string terminated) and the unicode61
      tokenizer treats U+0000 as a non-word separator, so `"a\0"`
      legitimately tokenizes to ["a"] and runs as a normal search.
      The relaxed invariant matches (1) — no panic, well-formed Ok
      or structured Err — and NUL is explicitly documented as a
      non-terminator.
  (3) fuzz_search_is_idempotent_under_repeated_calls — the same query
      run twice against the same seeded DB must yield identical
      (segment_id, score) lists on the Ok branch. Catches any hidden
      mutable state leaking between reads.

Local run: `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test storage_fts_fuzz
--no-default-features --features mcp,asupersync-runtime` completes in
~180s wall at /tmp/ft-pane6-target (~60s per property, 15,000 total
fuzz iterations). All three properties pass after the premise fix for
(2) landed in this same file.

Harness deliberately skips cargo-fuzz (needs nightly + fuzz crate
plumbing) in favor of proptest inside the normal cargo test loop —
structure-aware enough to reach the same grammar edges and runnable
on stable with zero extra toolchain.
